_meta.dataSource: Training knowledge (mid-2025). WebFetch/WebSearch denied in environment.

competitors.name: TopCV
competitors.layout: Full-width light theme with fixed white navbar, prominent hero search bar centered on homepage, 3-column job card grid on listing page, right-panel sticky filter sidebar. Footer is heavy with links.
competitors.typography: Roboto primary; headings bold 700-800; body 400-500; line-height ~1.6 Vietnamese-optimized
competitors.jobCardStyle: White card with subtle drop shadow, company logo left 40x40px, job title + company name + location stacked right, salary highlighted in green (#00B14F) badge bottom-left, 'Tin hot' yellow badge top-right when featured
competitors.searchFilterStyle: Dual-input hero bar (keyword + location) with green CTA. Listing page has left sidebar with accordion-style filters (industry, job type, salary range, experience level, work mode) using checkboxes; active filters shown as removable chips above results
strengths: Market leader in Vietnam — highest brand recognition
Comprehensive CV builder integrated into candidate dashboard
Rich filter system with saved search and email alerts
Employer branding pages with culture photos/videos
'Apply with TopCV profile' 1-click apply reduces friction
weaknesses: Visually dated — white/light theme with flat green feels 2017-era
Job card density low; salary often hidden behind 'Thỏa thuận'
Mobile experience laggy with poor touch targets
Dashboard cluttered with too many CTAs competing
No dark mode — eye strain on extended browsing
differentiators: Integrated CV builder with downloadable PDF templates
TopCV Score — AI-driven profile completeness scoring
Employer-side talent pool search with candidate ranking

competitors.name: VietnamWorks
competitors.layout: Wide fixed navbar (white/light gray), full-width hero with search bar, 2-column job listing grid on desktop (1-col mobile), prominent 'featured employer' horizontal scroll row, left sidebar filter on listing page
competitors.typography: Open Sans or Nunito (varies); headings 600-700; red #E41C38 brand/CTA; body dark gray #333
competitors.jobCardStyle: White card 1px gray border, company logo top-left 48x48px, job title bold link, company + location secondary text, salary in orange or 'Thỏa thuận', posted-date label, skill tags as small gray pills bottom
competitors.searchFilterStyle: Single hero search input with location dropdown. Listing filter sidebar uses collapsible sections — radio for single-select, checkbox for multi. 'Quick Apply' toggle filter for profile-only applications
strengths: Bilingual UI VI/EN — good for foreign companies hiring local
Premium job placement with visibility boost
weaknesses: Red primary (#E41C38) feels aggressive, inconsistent with modern job platform tone
UI density uneven — sparse on some pages, overwhelming on others
CV builder basic vs TopCV
No real-time chat between employer and candidate
Recommendation algorithm weak — mostly title match, not skills
differentiators: Quick Apply using saved profile (no CV upload needed)
Annual salary survey reports — credibility signal

competitors.name: ITviec
competitors.layout: Dark-accented navbar (#1A2332) with white content below; tech-focused niche; hero with keyword + city search; 2-col job listing grid with sticky filter panel; company profile pages rich with tech stack tags
competitors.typography: Inter or system-ui; strong tech aesthetic; tag/badge heavy; code-like monospace for tech stack labels; heading 700+
competitors.jobCardStyle: White card, left-aligned company logo 48x48px, job title bold red link on hover, tech skill tags as colored pills (per-language distinct color), salary range prominent in green, 'Hot'/'Urgent' badge overlay top-right, remote/onsite badge
competitors.searchFilterStyle: Keyword + location hero. Left filter panel with tech-skill tag cloud (clickable), company size, salary range slider, remote toggle. Filter state bookmarkable via URL params
strengths: Only major platform laser-focused on IT/tech — high signal-to-noise
Tech stack tag system on job cards — invaluable for engineers
Glassdoor-style company reviews integrated into employer profiles
Salary range displayed by default — no 'Thỏa thuận' evasion
weaknesses: Niche-only — useless for non-tech roles
Design functional but not premium; dark navy navbar clashes with white body
Mobile filter UX poor — drawer slides inconsistently
No skill-gap or match scoring shown to candidates
No status tracking dashboard — candidates rely on email
differentiators: Tech-tag filtering by specific stack (React, Node, Docker, etc.)
Salary transparency enforced — all listings show range

competitors.name: CareerBuilder VN
competitors.layout: Traditional portal: fixed white navbar, full-width hero banner (image background, search overlay), category icon grid below hero, 3-col job grid listing, right-sidebar ad space, heavy footer
competitors.typography: Arial/sans-serif fallback; headings blue #0055B3; body dark gray; little typographic hierarchy — most text feels same weight
competitors.jobCardStyle: White card 1px light gray border, small company logo top-left, job title blue link, company + location below, salary in orange if disclosed, 'Deadline: dd/mm/yyyy' in red bottom-right, urgency labels like 'Tuyển gấp' in red badge
competitors.searchFilterStyle: Hero search with keyword + industry dropdown + location dropdown + button. Listing filter horizontal tab-style above results (industry/job type/salary/level/age) plus secondary sidebar
strengths: Large job volume across all industries and seniority
Category browse grid intuitive for less tech-savvy users
weaknesses: Most visually outdated — feels 2012 portal
Age filter field is legally questionable, discrimination risk
Ad-heavy layout breaks visual hierarchy
Extremely slow page loads — heavy unoptimized assets
No skill matching, no profile scoring, no modern recommendations
Mobile site is unstyled desktop shrink
differentiators: Global brand affiliation credibility with large corporations

commonPatterns: White/light background with colored accent (red/green/blue) — all 4 are light theme
2-3 column job card grid on desktop, 1-col mobile
Left sidebar or top-row filter panel with collapsible sections
Company logo prominently top-left 40-48px on every job card
Hero search bar with keyword + location as two primary inputs
Salary either as range or hidden behind 'Thỏa thuận'
Fixed top navbar with logo + search + login/register CTAs
Saved jobs / bookmark on listing page
Application status tracking (PENDING / VIEWED / REJECTED minimum)

gapOpportunities.rationale: All 4 use white/light. Dark-first is differentiating, reduces eye strain on long browsing, appeals strongly to 18-35 tech demo. None even offer a dark toggle.

gapOpportunities.gap: Real-time match score on job cards
gapOpportunities.rationale: None show personalized match % on listing page. JobHub's Stage 11 score (skills 0.4 + certs 0.2 + exp 0.2 + prefs 0.2) as a badge on each card would be unique, immediately useful, reduces time-to-apply.

gapOpportunities.gap: Inline skill-gap analysis on saved/browsed jobs
gapOpportunities.rationale: ITviec shows tech tags but none say 'you are missing 2 of 5 required skills' with next steps. JobHub's gap analysis (Stage 11 P3) — competitors have zero equivalent.
ITviec has reviews only for tech. No VN platform offers employer reviews for general industries.
Only ITviec bans 'Thỏa thuận'. Enforcing salary display via form validation is candidate-trust-building differentiator.
All 4 are static — no scroll animations, hover states, transitions. Framer Motion scroll reveals create premium feel none offer.

gapOpportunities.gap: Certificate-based credential matching visible to candidates
gapOpportunities.rationale: No VN job platform surfaces required certifications on job cards or in filters. JobHub's cert dimension (Stage 11 P3) addresses a real pain point completely unmet.
